evidence_quotes.quote: a lot of feedback about games being remotely disabled by their sellers.

paragraphs.text: Dear members of the European Commission, I am enthusiastically sharing my positive feedback for the DFA. However I am still seeing a lot of feedback about games being remotely disabled by their sellers. While I do see this as a major issue for a lot of Europeans these days, I would like to broaden this concern. This remote disabling is also happening with other pieces of software. Some software products (notably, most of the Adobe software suite) require a verification server to be able to operate. However, when these software product are end-of-life the verification servers also go offline. Effectively destroying the software. This is in principle the same as what is happening with games (in a lot of cases), but we should also have a look at the broader software market. With kind regards
